request.question: Eric Estornel, better known by his stage names Maceo Plex and Maetrik, is an American DJ, producer and DJ Awards winner , he is best known for diverse production style, influenced primarily by house and techno but also tech house, as well as a longtime performer for many Ibiza-based clubs, including Space, was a Superclub on the island of Ibiza, Spain from 1986 to 2016, owned by who?.

title: Patrick Topping
Steve Lawler
Maceo Plex
Richie Hawtin
Sven Väth
Jamie Jones (DJ)
Space (Ibiza nightclub)
Roger Sanchez
Luciano (DJ)
Tech house
sentences: Patrick Topping (born 25 February 1989) is a British, Newcastle born, DJ and producer.
With releases on Hot Creations, Hottrax and Relief Records Patrick has hit Number 1's on the Beatport Tech House charts, and received DJ Awards for Newcomer DJ and Track Of The Season in Ibiza.

Steve Lawler is a British house music producer and DJ born in Birmingham, England he is a five time DJ Awards winner and has held residences at many popular dance clubs such as Space, The End and Twilo.
Because of his successful performances at Space in Ibiza, he was nicknamed "King of Space".
Lawler has released several mix albums and is especially well known for his "Lights Out" series featured on Boxed.
He currently runs the record label VIVa MUSiC.
He also founded the now defunct Harlem Records.
Eric Estornel, better known by his stage names Maceo Plex and Maetrik, is an American DJ, producer and DJ Awards winner from Dallas and Miami, United States, origin of Cuba.
He now resides in Barcelona, Spain with his wife and kids.
He is best known for diverse production style, influenced primarily by house and techno but also tech house, as well as a longtime performer for many Ibiza-based clubs, including Space and Amnesia.
Richard "Richie" Hawtin (born June 4, 1970) is a Canadian electronic musician and DJ and three-time DJ Awards winner.
He was an influential part of Detroit techno's second wave of artists in the early 1990s and has been a leading exponent of minimal techno since the mid-1990s.
He is best known for his work under the alias Plastikman and for his ENTER.
events in Ibiza and around the world.
Sven Väth (born 26 October 1964) is a German DJ/producer and three time DJ Awards winner whose career in electronic music spans well over 30 years.
His single, “Electrica Salsa” under the alias OFF launched his career in 1986.

Lovingly referred to as “Papa Sven” by his fans, Sven Väth has made his mark in the music community by being one of Germany’s “pop stars” in the nineties, running two famous night clubs in Germany, and starting his own company Cocoon, which encompasses a booking agency, record label, and a branch for events.

He is recognized for cultivating the underground electronic music scene not just in Germany but in Ibiza as well, with his own night at Amnesia for fourteen years and after-parties at creative locations around the island.
Sven Väth is a major proponent of vinyl, using only two decks and a mixer for his extensive DJ sets, his longest set having been 30 hours.
Jamie Jones is a Welsh DJ, producer and two time DJ Awards winner.
He is a member of the electronic music group Hot Natured and the head of the record label Hot Creations, as well as its sub-labels Emerald City and Hottrax.
Jones was voted the top DJ of 2011 by the online dance music magazine Resident Advisor.
In 2012 he received mainstream success with Hot Natured's single "Benediction", which reached number 31 in the UK Singles Chart.
Jones hosts his own "Paradise" party at Ibiza's DC10 nightclub.
In early 2016 Jones launched The House of Hot, an online shop clothing line, vinyls and artwork.
Space ( , ] , ] ) was a Superclub on the island of Ibiza, Spain from 1986 to 2016, owned by STANCA.
It was awarded "Best Global Club" at the International Dance Music Awards in 2005, 2006, 2012, and again in 2013.
"Space" was located in Playa d'en Bossa on the outskirts of Ibiza Town, close to the airport.
Roger Rene Sanchez (born June 1, 1967) is an American house music DJ, Remixer and Grammy Award Winner for his remix of "Hella Good" by No Doubt in 2003.
He is a four time DJ Awards winner for "Best House DJ" in 1999, 2002, 2004 and 2007 and has received twelve nominations in all.
He won the first International Dance Music Award for Best Podcast in 2007 and has received 8 IDMA nominations for Best American DJ (2003–2010).
Luciano (born Lucien Nicolet on February 24, 1978 in Montcherand, Switzerland) is a Chilean-Swiss DJ and producer he is a five-time DJ Awards winner and ten-time nominee.

He is part of a small group of people who were responsible for developing and nurturing the underground electronic music scene in Chile, via promoting, playing, and throwing parties in the Chilean club circuit.
His DJ sets are strongly influenced by Latin American music, a sound he heavily incorporates.
Over the years, Luciano's initial style of minimal techno has developed and evolved into a blend of house and techno.
Luciano has been constantly involved in various music projects, such as playing live performances, running his label Cadenza Records, and hosting multiple parties in Ibiza.
Tech house is a subgenre of house music that mixes elements of techno with house.

The term "tech house" developed as a shorthand record store name for a category of electronic dance music that combined musical aspects of techno, such as "rugged basslines" and "steely beats," with the harmonies and grooves of progressive house.
The music originally had a clean and minimal production style that was associated with techno from Detroit and the UK.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Task: Compress the grounded first-hop evidence into a single concise, retrieval-oriented intermediate state to guide the next-hop retrieval. Do not produce the final answer or perform any new retrieval here.

Required content of `summary` (single field):
- A short header identifying the primary topic(s) found in the passages (1 line).
- A concise enumeration of the concrete facts supported verbatim by the provided passages: list entity mentions and directly supported factual spans (dates, years, titles, roles). Use very short quoted fragments (<= ~15 words) only when those words appear verbatim in the passages. Keep to facts present in the passages; do not add or infer new facts.
- Explicitly expose any ambiguity or missing information that blocks answering: if multiple candidate entities or page variants appear, enumerate those candidates and label the name as ambiguous; if a required target fact is missing or ambiguous, label it as the missing fact and identify the bridge entity that connects this hop to the next.
- Prioritized next-hop retrieval directives (1–4 items): concrete page titles or disambiguation pages to fetch, explicit article sections to inspect (e.g., lede/infobox, "Career", "Publication", "History"), or tight search keywords. Order them by priority and be specific (include possible disambiguation terms or section names).

Formatting and style constraints:
- Keep the entire `summary` concise: a short header plus 1–4 brief sentences or 2–6 short bullet lines in total.
- Preserve the habit of extracting and citing concrete facts verbatim from the passages (names, dates, passage labels).
- Do not output a final answer, comparison, or any numeric fact that purports to resolve the question here. Even if the passages fully and unambiguously contain all required facts, present that evidence and explicitly state "no additional retrieval needed," but defer producing any final answer to a downstream component.
- If a name or term has multiple distinct variants in the passages, do not pick one without disambiguating evidence; enumerate candidates instead.
- Do not invent or add facts not present in the supplied passages. Avoid long verbatim quotations; prefer short extracts (<= ~15 words) or paraphrase.

Output expectations: produce exactly one field `summary` containing the concise, retrieval-oriented summary that meets the above requirements.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact, retrieval-focused second-hop query in the field `query`. Use only the provided `question` and `summary_1` as inputs. Do not include any explanation, answer, fallback text, lists, or additional fields — the output must be a single one-line query string.

When composing the query, select the minimal anchor token(s) visible in `question` and/or `summary_1` that point to the missing page or fact. You may append a single short attribute keyword (for example country member occupations record season) only when that keyword is needed to disambiguate the evidence type. Do not introduce any other new tokens.

Decision templates (choose the single template that matches the missing evidence implied by summary_1):
- Connector between two named items: emit a short interrogative naming both items and requesting the linking evidence (e.g., artist album recording) in one line. Retain the visible item tokens from the inputs.
- Metadata for a single anchor title: emit a compact keyword-style query consisting of the anchor plus brief attribute keywords (for example: artist genre).
- Temporal or aggregate fact: include the year or season token plus the entity type and a short cue such as season count.

Use these operational rules when choosing a template:
- If summary_1 explicitly lists two or more distinct page anchors as next-hop targets, combine those visible anchor tokens into one compact multi-anchor query and append a short attribute keyword naming the evidence type (for example country member occupations record).
- If summary_1 flags a single missing anchor or a missing_titles list is present, emit a single-anchor metadata-style query that places the missing anchor token first and appends a concise attribute keyword naming the desired fact.
- When summary_1 names multiple candidate entities and the missing fact concerns one candidate’s membership, order, or relation to a canonical collection, prefer a connector-style query that pairs the highest-priority candidate token (first or prioritized in summary_1) with the canonical collection token plus a short attribute keyword requesting membership or order.
- When individual member tokens are present but the needed retrieval is about that individual, prefer member-token + group-anchor + member (or equivalent short attribute) to target the individual’s page rather than issuing a broad group-level query.

Strict formatting rules:
- Emit exactly one line and exactly one string in `query` containing only the query text.
- Keep the query compact (aim for ≤ 10–12 words/tokens) and free of punctuation or explanatory clauses.
- Preserve visible tokens from `question` and `summary_1` (title words, years, or entity names) to anchor retrieval.
- Do not answer, conclude, enumerate candidates, assert hidden facts, or include user-facing instructions.

Produce the `query` field now.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Instructions (must follow exactly):
- Emit a single field named `summary` containing only 1–3 concise sentences that integrate the first-hop `context` and the second-hop `passages` into a grounded post-retrieval state.
- If the `context` already contains a concise answer sentence, keep that sentence as the first sentence of `summary` unless the `passages` clearly contradict it; append supporting sentence(s) after it rather than replacing it.
- Preserve the primary answer name(s) exactly as they appear in the inputs (context or passages) unless those names clearly conflict with stronger passage content.
- When one or more `passages` supply short biographical, membership, partnership, or corroborating facts about a named entity from the `context`, append at most one brief supporting sentence per relevant named person. Each such supporting sentence should:
  - begin with the entity's canonical name as shown in the passages (if available),
  - paraphrase up to one or two key attributes present in the passages (examples: nationality, award/winner status, current affiliation/team, partner identity, founding/formation fact, birth date), and
  - avoid long verbatim copying (prefer paraphrase; use short quotes only when a brief exact phrase from a passage is necessary for clarity).
- If the question requires naming both members of a partnership or collaborator pair, name both people in the summary and, if passages provide partner-specific attributes, include one brief attribute for each as separate short clauses or sentences if needed.
- Do not invent facts not present in the provided passages. If passages conflict on a specific attribute, do not synthesize a new reconciled fact; instead prefer the passage that most directly addresses the question or omit the disputed attribute (or state necessary ambiguity only when needed to avoid a false claim).
- Keep the `summary` compact and focused: avoid extraneous biography, discography/chart data, or unrelated commentary. When `context` is absent or is not a concise answer, produce a 1–2 sentence integrated summary naming the relevant entity(ies) and including at most one brief paraphrased attribute drawn from the passages.

Additional constraints (must follow):
- The first sentence must be an answer-first concise identification (a direct answer or concise identification). If the `context` already contains such a sentence, preserve it exactly unless a passage explicitly and directly contradicts it; otherwise derive that first sentence from the clearest passage.
- After the first sentence, append at most one short supporting sentence for each additional relevant named entity only when the passages supply corroborating attributes. Each supporting sentence must begin with the entity's canonical name as shown in the inputs (context or passages) and paraphrase no more than one or two simple attributes.
- When a queried binary/status attribute for an entity is not stated in the provided passages, indicate that absence concisely within that entity's supporting sentence (for example, with a short parenthetical or clause saying the status is not given in the provided evidence).
- If the passages explicitly assert a relationship between two or more entities that is directly relevant to the question, include one short relationship sentence after the per-entity supporting sentence(s) describing that relationship; do not embed the same relationship claim redundantly in multiple sentences.
- If relevant entities exceed the available supporting-sentence budget under the 1–3 sentence limit, prioritize entities in this order: entities explicitly named in the question, entities named in the preserved first sentence, then other context entities. Do not exceed the 1–3 sentence cap.

Output requirement: exactly one JSON field `summary` whose value is the integrated 1–3 sentence summary.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the fields `answer`.

Instructions:
- Use only `summary_1` and `summary_2` as the evidential basis for the final decision; do not add new facts or external information.
- First determine whether the question is a binary (yes/no) proposition and whether both `summary_1` and `summary_2` clearly and unambiguously support the same binary outcome.
- If BOTH summaries unambiguously assert the same binary conclusion, emit exactly one lowercase token as the entire content of `answer`: use "yes" for an affirmative conclusion and "no" for a negative conclusion. The `answer` field must contain that single token and nothing else (no punctuation, no extra whitespace, no explanation).
- Prefer `summary_2` as the decisive statement when it clarifies or restates `summary_1`, but still require that both summaries support the same binary judgment before emitting the single-token `answer`.
- If the summaries conflict, if either summary hedges, is ambiguous, silent about the binary relation, or otherwise fails to clearly agree, do NOT apply the single-token rule; instead produce the module's normal fallback/unresolved output for `answer`.
- Do not infer that the final state is unresolved merely because `summary_1` uses earlier unresolved or bridge language; prefer `summary_2` when it clarifies `summary_1`.
- Always remain truthful to the visible summaries and do not alter their factual conclusions.

Binary detection is mandatory and must run first. Only if the question is not a binary yes/no or the binary detector does not apply, proceed to the extractive rules below. Use the extractor that matches the question intent and only apply an extractor when the summaries present the required adjacent or verbatim phrasing.

Extraction and formatting rules (apply only when binary detection does not produce a single-token answer):
- Person-name selection: if a longer verbatim personal-name string appears in either summary and contains a shorter candidate as a substring, emit the longest verbatim name exactly as it appears in the summaries. Prefer wording from `summary_2` when both summaries provide the same evidence but differ in form.
- Denomination/type extraction: for questions asking a "kind", "type", or "denomination" of a role, extract and emit exactly the minimal token or short phrase that appears immediately adjacent to the role word in the summaries (the word/phrase answering "what kind").
- Event-name extraction: when summaries show a contiguous proper-name followed by a generic event-type noun, emit only the contiguous proper-name core. Strip any leading articles and remove the trailing generic event-type noun; emit only the proper-name portion exactly as given in the summaries.
- Nationality handling: if both summaries unambiguously state the same nationality phrase and at least one summary places the literal single word "citizen" immediately after that nationality phrase, append exactly one space and the single word "citizen" to the nationality phrase. Otherwise emit the nationality phrase exactly as given in the summaries.

General formatting constraints for all outputs:
- Always emit the minimal answer string supported by the visible summaries and do not add explanation, context, punctuation, or extra words.
- Do not introduce external facts, additional descriptors, dates, roles, or explanatory text not present in the summaries.
- Do not normalize or invent name variants that are not verbatim in the summaries.
- If the summaries conflict, lack the required adjacent phrasing, or do not present a clear verbatim form for the requested attribute, do not guess—use the module's normal unresolved output.

Output contract:
- Produce a single field `answer` whose contents follow the rules above. The `answer` field must contain only the minimal string supported by `summary_1` and `summary_2` and no additional tokens, punctuation, or explanation.

response.hop1_query: Eric Estornel, better known by his stage names Maceo Plex and Maetrik, is an American DJ, producer and DJ Awards winner , he is best known for diverse production style, influenced primarily by house and techno but also tech house, as well as a longtime performer for many Ibiza-based clubs, including Space, was a Superclub on the island of Ibiza, Spain from 1986 to 2016, owned by who?.
hop1_docs: Maceo Plex | Eric Estornel, better known by his stage names Maceo Plex and Maetrik, is an American DJ, producer and DJ Awards winner from Dallas and Miami, United States, origin of Cuba.  He now resides in Barcelona, Spain with his wife and kids.  He is best known for diverse production style, influenced primarily by house and techno but also tech house, as well as a longtime performer for many Ibiza-based clubs, including Space and Amnesia.

Space (Ibiza nightclub) | Space ( , ] , ] ) was a Superclub on the island of Ibiza, Spain from 1986 to 2016, owned by STANCA.  It was awarded "Best Global Club" at the International Dance Music Awards in 2005, 2006, 2012, and again in 2013.  "Space" was located in Playa d'en Bossa on the outskirts of Ibiza Town, close to the airport.

Steve Lawler | Steve Lawler is a British house music producer and DJ born in Birmingham, England he is a five time DJ Awards winner and has held residences at many popular dance clubs such as Space, The End and Twilo.  Because of his successful performances at Space in Ibiza, he was nicknamed "King of Space".  Lawler has released several mix albums and is especially well known for his "Lights Out" series featured on Boxed.  He currently runs the record label VIVa MUSiC.  He also founded the now defunct Harlem Records.

Luciano (DJ) | Luciano (born Lucien Nicolet on February 24, 1978 in Montcherand, Switzerland) is a Chilean-Swiss DJ and producer he is a five-time DJ Awards winner and ten-time nominee.  He is part of a small group of people who were responsible for developing and nurturing the underground electronic music scene in Chile, via promoting, playing, and throwing parties in the Chilean club circuit.  His DJ sets are strongly influenced by Latin American music, a sound he heavily incorporates.  Over the years, Luciano's initial style of minimal techno has developed and evolved into a blend of house and techno.  Luciano has been constantly involved in various music projects, such as playing live performances, running his label Cadenza Records, and hosting multiple parties in Ibiza.

Switch (house DJ) | David James Andrew Taylor, better known his stage name Switch, is a British DJ, songwriter, sound engineer, and record producer.  He is best known for his work with M.I.A..  In the fidget house genre, Switch runs his own music label "Dubsided", as well as the label Counterfeet, established in 2006 with fellow producer Sinden.  He has released various singles under his own name, and is also well known for remixing and producing for many major artists.  He is a former member of the American electronic dancehall group Major Lazer.

Onur Sasmaz | Onur Sasmaz, commonly known by his stage name DJ Onur or DJ Sasmaz, is a Turkish DJ/producer.  He is best known for the live performing.  Starting out with techno and subsequently moving to hardstyle, they eventually worked in other EDM styles including progressive/electro house.

Thomas Gandey | Thomas Gandey is an English electronic music producer, vocalist, keyboard player and DJ born on 21 March 1976, known for work under his own name as well as under his "Cagedbaby" pseudonym.  He has remixed the likes of Shirley Bassey, Ocean Colour Scene, Empire of the Sun, Fatboy Slim, The Temper Trap and Grace Jones and DJed and performed at many of the world's best-known clubs and festivals, including Womb, Glastonbury Festival, Fuji Rock Festival, and Tokyo's Big Beach Festival.  He has been a resident at We Love Space Ibiza and Barcelona's Razzmatazz.
response.summary_1:
Primary topics: Maceo Plex (Eric Estornel) and Space (Ibiza nightclub)

- From passage [1]: Eric Estornel "better known by his stage names Maceo Plex and Maetrik"; "American DJ, producer and DJ Awards winner"; "longtime performer ... including Space and Amnesia."
- From passage [2]: Space "(...) was a Superclub on the island of Ibiza, Spain from 1986 to 2016"; located in "Playa d'en Bossa"; "owned by STANCA"; awarded "Best Global Club" in 2005, 2006, 2012, and 2013.
- Ambiguity/missing facts: owner is explicitly given as "STANCA" in passage [2]; no alternate owners or contradicting owner names appear in the provided passages — no additional ambiguity present.
- Next-hop retrieval directives (priority order):
  1. Space (Ibiza nightclub) — inspect lede/infobox and "History" section to confirm owner and ownership timeline.
  2. STANCA — fetch page or search for "STANCA company Ibiza Space owner" to verify entity details and ownership role.
  3. Maceo Plex (Eric Estornel) — check "Career" or "Performances" sections for links/mentions of Space residency (optional verification).

No additional retrieval needed to answer the direct question about Space's owner in these passages: passage [2] states it was "owned by STANCA."
hop2_docs: Space (Ibiza nightclub) | Space ( , ] , ] ) was a Superclub on the island of Ibiza, Spain from 1986 to 2016, owned by STANCA.  It was awarded "Best Global Club" at the International Dance Music Awards in 2005, 2006, 2012, and again in 2013.  "Space" was located in Playa d'en Bossa on the outskirts of Ibiza Town, close to the airport.

Ibiza (town) | Ibiza (Catalan: "Eivissa" ] ; Spanish: "Ibiza" ), also known as Ibiza Town, is a city and municipality located on the southeast coast of the island of Ibiza in the Balearic Islands autonomous community.

Jesús (Ibiza) | Nostra Senyora de Jesús is a village on the northern side of Ibiza Bay, On the Spanish island of Ibiza, Spain.  The village is in the municipality of Santa Eulària des Riu and is located on the designated road PMV 810.  The village is 2.0 mi North of Ibiza Town and 6.7 mi of Ibiza Airport.

Ibiza, Ibiza | Ibiza, Ibiza is a 1986 Welsh comedy television film which aired on Welsh-language channel S4C.  It was directed by Ronw Protheroe and stars Caryl Parry Jones, Siw Hughes, Emyr Wyn, and Huw Chiswell.  It covers the stories of three women as they go on holiday to the island of Ibiza.

Pacte Progressista | The Pacte Progressista (English: Progressive Pact , Pacte), officially the Pacte Progressista d'Eivissa (English: Progressive Pact of Ibiza ) was an electoral alliance formed in Ibiza by the Socialist Party of the Balearic Islands, United Left of Ibiza, The Greens, Entesa Nacionalista i Ecologista (English: Nationalist and Ecologist Agreement of Ibiza ) and Republican Left of Catalonia to contest the 1999 and 2003 regional elections.  Previously, it had contested the 1996 Senate election in both Ibiza and Formentera as the Ibiza and Formentera in the Senate (Catalan: "Eivissa i Formentera al Senat" ).

Ibiza Weekender | Ibiza Weekender is a British reality television series following a group of 18-24 year olds spending a weekend at a hotel resort on the island of Ibiza, Spain.  Previously, the show was named The Magaluf Weekender and was located at Lively Hotel in Magaluf, located on the Balearic Island of Mallorca, from Series 1–3.  In Series 4, the show relocated to Ibiza, and was renamed "Ibiza Weekender".

SE Eivissa-Ibiza B | Societat Esportiva Eivissa-Ibiza B was a football team based in Ibiza Town in the autonomous community of Balearic Islands.  Founded in 2005, it plays in the Preferente.  It was the reserve team of UD Ibiza-Eivissa.
response.summary_2: Space was a Superclub on the island of Ibiza, Spain from 1986 to 2016, owned by STANCA. Eric Estornel (better known as Maceo Plex) is an American DJ and producer who has been a longtime performer at Ibiza clubs including Space.
